title: Day 1: The Freelancer Mindset
content: Theory: Understanding the shift from employee to owner. Managing risk, taxes, and time.
exercise: Exercise: List 3 "Employee Habits" (e.g., waiting for instructions) you need to drop this month.

title: Day 2: Niche Selection Logic
content: Theory: Specialization vs. Generalization. Why "the riches are in the niches."
exercise: Exercise: List 10 industries. Use Google Trends to narrow it down to 2 that have high demand right now.

title: Day 3: The Irresistible Offer
content: Theory: Selling a "Solution," not a "Service." People pay for results, not hours.
exercise: Exercise: Write your offer: "I help [Target] achieve [Result] through [Skill] so they can [Benefit]."

title: Day 4: Pricing Strategies
content: Theory: Hourly vs. Flat Fee vs. Value-Based. How to stop trading time for money.
exercise: Exercise: Calculate your "Minimum Acceptable Rate" (MAR) covering your bills + 30% for taxes.

title: Day 5: Competitive Research
content: Theory: Mapping the market. What are others in your niche charging and offering?
exercise: Exercise: Find 5 freelancers on LinkedIn/Upwork. Note one "weakness" in their profiles you can beat.

title: Day 6: The "Spec" Portfolio
content: Theory: How to show proof when you have no clients. Building "Case Studies" from scratch.
exercise: Exercise: Create 1 "Before & After" case study for a fictional project in your chosen niche.

title: Day 7: Optimizing the Bio
content: Theory: Turning your social media profiles into "Sales Pages."
exercise: Exercise: Rewrite your LinkedIn/X headline to focus on the client's problem, not your "Skills."

title: Day 8: Legal & Registration
content: Theory: Sole Proprietorship vs. LLC. Protecting your personal assets.
exercise: Exercise: Research the exact legal steps to register as a freelancer in your specific country/state.

title: Day 9: Financial Setup
content: Theory: Business vs. Personal money. Why you need a separate bank account today.
exercise: Exercise: Open a separate "Business" sub-account and create a simple sheet for expense tracking.

title: Day 10: Contract Essentials
content: Theory: The "Scope of Work" and "Payment Terms." Preventing "Scope Creep" before it starts.
exercise: Exercise: Find a standard freelance contract online. Highlight the "Termination" and "Payment" clauses.

title: Day 11: Invoicing Systems
content: Theory: Making it easy for people to pay you. Using tools like Stripe, PayPal, or Wise.
exercise: Exercise: Create a professional invoice template with your logo, payment links, and "Late Fee" policy.

title: Day 12: Project Management Tools
content: Theory: Organizing your workload. Using Notion or Trello to track project stages.
exercise: Exercise: Set up a board with 4 columns: "Inquiry," "In Progress," "Review," and "Paid."

title: Day 13: Setting Boundaries
content: Theory: Avoiding burnout. Defining "Office Hours" and communication rules for clients.
exercise: Exercise: Write a "Welcome Packet" (PDF or Email) that tells clients exactly how and when you work.

title: Day 14: Time Tracking Mastery
content: Theory: Knowing where your time goes. Is your "Hourly Rate" actually $0 because of distractions?
exercise: Exercise: Use a tool (Toggl/Clockify) to track every minute of your "Skill Learning" hour today.

title: Day 15: Inbound vs. Outbound
content: Theory: Attracting vs. Hunting. Why you need content and outreach to survive.
exercise: Exercise: Write 1 "Authority" post sharing a tip and find 5 potential clients to contact.

title: Day 16: The Cold Pitch Script
content: Theory: High-converting outreach. Stop saying "Hire me" and start saying "I saw this gap."
exercise: Exercise: Draft a 3-sentence cold pitch email. Sentence 2 must be a specific observation about their business.

title: Day 17: Warm Network Outreach
content: Theory: Tapping into your existing contacts. Low-hanging fruit for first projects.
exercise: Exercise: Send 10 "Checking In" messages to former colleagues/friends announcing your new service.

title: Day 18: Upwork/Contra Strategy
content: Theory: Winning on platforms without "racing to the bottom" on price.
exercise: Exercise: Optimize 1 platform profile. Use your "Offer Statement" from Day 3 as the main headline.

title: Day 19: The Discovery Call
content: Theory: Leading the meeting. How to ask "Pain Questions" that make the client close themselves.
exercise: Exercise: Write 5 "Discovery Questions" (e.g., "What happens if this problem doesn't get fixed?").

title: Day 20: The Winning Proposal
content: Theory: Turning a talk into a deal. Structure: Problem > Solution > ROI > Next Steps.
exercise: Exercise: Create a 1-page proposal template based on your Day 6 "Spec Project."

title: Day 21: The Follow-up Loop
content: Theory: The fortune is in the follow-up. How to stay top-of-mind without being annoying.
exercise: Exercise: Create a 14-day follow-up schedule (e.g., Day 2: Value link, Day 7: Case study, Day 14: Final check).

title: Day 22: Onboarding Systems
content: Theory: The "Golden Hour." The first 24 hours of a client project defines the relationship.
exercise: Exercise: Create an "Onboarding Checklist" (Contract signed, Deposit paid, Access granted).

title: Day 23: Managing Scope Creep
content: Theory: How to say "No" and get paid more for extra work politely.
exercise: Exercise: Write a script for when a client asks for "one small extra edit" that isn't in the contract.

title: Day 24: Milestone Delivery
content: Theory: Professionalism through punctuality. Breaking big tasks into mini-wins for the client.
exercise: Exercise: Break your Day 6 project into 3 milestones. Set internal deadlines 2 days before the "final."

title: Day 25: Gathering Testimonials
content: Theory: Closing the growth loop. Why you should ask for a review before the project even ends.
exercise: Exercise: Create a "Feedback Form" (Google Form/Typeform) with 3 questions that lead to a 5-star quote.

title: Day 26: Upselling Services
content: Theory: Retainers vs. One-offs. How to make more from the same client.
exercise: Exercise: Identify one "Add-on" service for your niche (e.g., Video Editor offering Thumbnail Design).

title: Day 27: Freelance Communities
content: Theory: Why you shouldn't freelance alone. Networking for referrals and support.
exercise: Exercise: Join one Slack, Discord, or Facebook group for freelancers in your specific skill.

title: Day 28: The "Lean" Fund
content: Theory: Managing the "Feast or Famine" cycle. Preparing for the slow months.
exercise: Exercise: Calculate your "Emergency Fund" goal (3 months of living expenses) and set a monthly savings target.

title: Day 29: Reviewing the "L's"
content: Theory: Auditing your failures. Why did those last 3 leads say "No"?
exercise: Exercise: Review every "No" you got this month. Identify the single biggest bottleneck (e.g., Price, Trust, or Niche).

title: Day 30: The 90-Day Roadmap
content: Theory: Strategy for the next quarter. Setting revenue and lead-gen targets.
exercise: Exercise: Set 3 goals for the next 90 days (e.g., "Hit $X in Revenue," "Get 1 Retainer Client").
